subject: Happy Twitterversary

body:

Back then, who would have thought a President would use it to fire people and attack Americans. [_**Join me and tell President Trump: #DeleteYourAccount**_](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113fe3/622b7b5b/19c0dabe/307b8da1/2206275899/VEsE/ "https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/e180321")
Thank you,
Debbie

subject: Pledge to protect voting rights
body:
Add your name to this NAACP LDF pledge.

(http://democrats.pages.ontraport.net/votingrights)

WILL YOU DEFEND OUR DEMOCRACY?

PLEDGE NOW (http://democrats.pages.ontraport.net/votingrights)

Activist, in this critical midterm election year, one thing is certain: we must protect our right to vote.

Pledge to stand with the NAACP Legal Defense and Educational Fund Inc. (LDF) in support of voting rights now. (http://democrats.pages.ontraport.net/votingrights)

LDF has led the fight for voting rights since our founding nearly 80 years ago. One of our first victories, won by LDF founder Thurgood Marshall, was in Smith v. Allwright. This landmark Supreme Court case ended whites-only primaries in Texas and signaled the beginning of the modern civil rights movement.

More recently, when the heart of the Voting Rights Act of 1965 was gutted in 2013, we increased our efforts to protect voters across the country from disenfranchisement and discrimination.

Unfortunately, efforts to silence voters, particularly voters of color, have only increased – which is why we need justice-minded people like you to help defend our democracy in the months ahead.

(http://democrats.pages.ontraport.net/votingrights)

The current Administration has worked to suppress the vote by spreading false information about voter fraud, and even creating an “Election Integrity Commission” to investigate it. In response, LDF filed a lawsuit (LDF v. Trump) arguing the commission was designed specifically to suppress African-American and Latino votes.

Thanks to litigation pressure from LDF and other civil rights groups the commission was recently disbanded.

But the threat to the vote has not gone away – and with midterm elections approaching, now is the time to stand together and challenge these persistent attacks.

Help us fight back before it’s too late. Pledge to stand up for voting rights – and to exercise your own right to vote – now. (http://democrats.pages.ontraport.net/votingrights)

With you in struggle, Sherrilyn A. Ifill
President and Director-Counsel
(http://democrats.pages.ontraport.net/votingrights)

150 years since its enactment, the 14th Amendment continues to do the heavy lifting in the fight for equal access to education, housing, and employment. Your pledge today (http://democrats.pages.ontraport.net/votingrights) is a show of commitment to continuing our work for equality for all.

name: The White House
subject: Targeting China’s economic aggression
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=9280b889c29bd4ea1916928f07cd8371a81541400d3f658a0353d0d5da92dc58cb88b03d441794e858337ab3229a17bffbdb3cf6b88cacc8

Targeting China’s economic aggression

In the White House Diplomatic Reception Room yesterday, President Trump made it clear he’s taking a stand for American innovation.

“We’ve lost, over a fairly short period of time, 60,000 factories in our country—closed, shuttered, gone. Six million jobs, at least, gone. And now they’re starting to come back,” the President said. “But we have one particular problem. And I view them as a friend . . . and that’s China.”

The President has directed his Administration to consider a range of actions to respond to China’s unfair and harmful acquisition of U.S. technology. Now, the Administration is proposing for public comment adding 25 percent additional tariffs on products that are supported by China’s unfair industrial policy.

“The word that I want to use is ‘reciprocal,’” President Trump said. “When they charge 25 percent for a car to go in, and we charge 2 percent for their car to come into the United States, that’s not good.”

Watch:
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=9280b889c29bd4eadcae20e1601c37f7d3ef6ea7daf8eb9974af503b49d02be01fc51895bd10ef01ad8dc00c9d3bc5918b49a9ca17024bcd
President Trump signs a Memorandum targeting China’s economic aggression

Learn more:
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=9280b889c29bd4ea642ddc67c89f799c5f277ada728db7022e745310970ab7621a170ee13982b32e34bdf298250231029ca30a22d430e983
How we must level the playing field for American workers

What advice would you give?

Americans born between 1982 and 2000 represent more than a quarter of our Nation’s population. These millennials now outnumber baby boomers, and they’re becoming a growing force in our country’s policy debates.

A booming economy, more jobs, and free speech protections—especially on America’s college campuses—are just some of the ways the Trump Administration is helping millennials. Yesterday, the White House hosted about 150 of these young men and women for panel discussions and an interview with President Trump.

Charlie Kirk, founder of the conservative nonprofit Turning Point USA, asked the President questions about taxes, trade policy, and free speech. But the question that sparked the most reaction? “What advice would you give to the 25-year-old Donald Trump, knowing what you know today?”

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=9280b889c29bd4ea3674daec2b7416d3fd6423f346e9aa3b943a12197672f23adce55ff27aa348e78c11c6e43b540fc9817982b8a4713c82
Watch Charlie Kirk’s full interview with President Trump here.

Photo of the Day

President Donald J. Trump and Archbishop Demetrios at the Greek Independence Day celebration | March 22, 2018 (Official White House Photo by Joyce N. Boghosian)

POTUS TODAY

This morning, President Trump and Vice President Pence will meet with the Secretary of Defense, the Secretary of Homeland Security, and the Deputy Secretary of State.

This afternoon, the Vice President will travel to Atlanta, Georgia, to deliver keynote remarks at an America First Policies “Tax Cuts to Put America First” event.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=adf0b8590ff5e40f8bf54c425048853741c2514afc39fd36e9ebc21efe94972984e0b6991b46094219cccca0a965d2bd2b9645241f0cad96
Privacy Policy |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=adf0b8590ff5e40f20751fc6047d1d808334d2bc421f4fd66dc750e8cbcaa5ca450e286cc7502ca1c11c55ab53354eb3e4b6f8c5b1fd3d0b
Contact the White House |

name: Tim Kaine
subject: A (politics-free!) update from the trail
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
Derek:

As you know, our campaign has been ramping up recently - and we're about to crank into high gear. Over the coming weeks and months, I'll be doing even more traveling around the Commonwealth to share our message of equality and progress with as many Virginians as possible. And as much as I love these opportunities to directly connect with and hear from so many members of our community, life on the road can also be pretty tiring.

The silver lining is that I've learned how to make the most of my travel time - hitting the road gives me a chance to throw some new albums into my regular rotation.

Today, I wanted to take a few minutes to share with you what I'm reading and listening to in the car. If you haven't heard of these, check 'em out. **_What I 'm reading:_** Two books I haven't been able to put down lately are **" Bury the Chains,"** by Adam Hochschild, and **" Killers of the Flower Moon,"** by David Grann.

"Bury the Chains," came out a while ago and it's an amazing history of the abolitionist movement in Britain. So much of what we do in grassroots campaigns - from lapel pins to newsletters and fundraising letters - directly follows the work these abolitionists were doing, all the way back in the 1780s. It's fascinating.

So is "Killers of the Flower Moon," a stunner that dives deep into the Osage murders of the 1920s - murders whose investigation gave rise to the FBI as we know it. I don't want to say too much about it, because the author does such a great job laying out his own investigation - which turns up some shocking and heartbreaking information. I'd recommend both of these books to anyone who, like me, loves to learn about history from new, unexamined angles. **_What I'm listening to:_** I'm a huge fan of **The Replacements** , an alt-rock band I fell in love with in the 80s. And they were famous for their concerts, which would often be genius ... but sometimes their punk-rock antics would drown out the music they were actually playing.

Recently, somebody uncovered a tape of what was regarded as one of their best shows - at Maxwell's in 1986 - and a record label released the live performance. As soon as I got this two-CD album, it was instantly added to my playlist.

The Replacements might not be for everyone, but if you're into that punk-style, the early foundations of alternative rock, you should definitely give this a listen.

**Trust me on this:** No matter how busy your life gets, you should always try to make time to relax and enjoy something new. Let me know if you find something interesting I should check out! _I 'm always looking for recommendations._

Thank you,

_Tim_

name: John Kasich
subject: RE: $1,300,000,000,000 📈
body:
Now more than ever, we need to make our voices heard. Enough is
enough. This will add hundreds of billions to our runaway
national debt and it only funds the federal government until
September!

Peter, $1.3 Trillion - That's
how large the spending bill is that Congress just passed and the
President signed.

This will add hundreds of
billions to our runaway national debt and it only funds the
federal government until September!

Now more than ever, we need to
make our voices heard. Enough is enough!

Can I count on your support?

Thanks for all that you're doing.

-John

--

From: John Kasch
Date: March 20, 2018
Subject: It happened again

On Thursday, the United States
hit another important milestone.

Our national debt officially
crossed $21 TRILLION and counting - and we're on pace to reach
$22 trillion in a year. We hit $20 trillion just last September
and $19 trillion in 2016!

Durham, N.H. Town Hall - 2/3/16

That is a staggering
mismanagement of our government and future generations of
Americans will pay dearly for the negligence of our elected
leaders!

Peter, our national debt isn't
making many news headlines these days, though it should. It is up
to you, me and citizens all over this country to demand that our
elected leaders in Washington wake up to not only the economic
risks of this but the geopolitical risks of borrowing endlessly
from China and other nations that may not share our values.

I did the best I could to raise
this issue during the 2016 campaign but we must ramp up our
efforts to draw attention to this critical issue. Can I count on
your support?

The funds we raise go directly
into the tools we use to carry this message and others that are
getting more and more attention every day. Please consider
chipping in whatever you can to help.

Thank you for all that you're
doing.

-John

Paid for by Kasich
for America, not authorized by any candidate or candidate's
committee.

Despite booming production, Pennsylvania is the only major gas-producing state in the country without a severance tax on natural gas.

That's because natural gas drillers are spending more than $60 million on lobbyists.

But Governor Wolf is taking on the lobbyists and special interests -- and demanding a severance tax.

**[Learn more in our new ad, then pitch in $10 or whatever you can to help us keep it on the air:](https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/m/342ecfa5/73a2bc12/3fbf534c/1fcce813/75653892/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wolf-ad-lobbyist?utm_campaign=em18_180323_newad_fnd_clq_act_lob_js&utm_medium=email&utm_source=wolf&source=em18_180323_newad_fnd_clq_act_lob_js&noskip=true&recurring=auto&amount=25&utm_content=2+-+Learn+more+in+our+new+ad+then+pitch+in+1")** [](https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/m/342ecfa5/73a2bc12/3fbf534c/1fcce812/75653892/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wolf-ad-lobbyist?utm_campaign=em18_180323_newad_fnd_clq_act_lob_js&utm_medium=email&utm_source=wolf&source=em18_180323_newad_fnd_clq_act_lob_js&noskip=true&recurring=auto&amount=25&utm_content=3")

Every day Governor Wolf is working to change Harrisburg for all of us -- and he needs our help to keep fighting.

clean_body: \-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- "" Hello -- Despite booming production, Pennsylvania is the only major gas-producing state in the country without a severance tax on natural gas. That's because natural gas drillers are spending more than $60 million on lobbyists. But Governor Wolf is taking on the lobbyists and special interests -- and demanding a severance tax. "" "" Harrisburg politicians are siding with special interests over Pennsylvanians. They want to continue to let the oil and gas companies off the hook. Governor Wolf wants to see Pennsylvanians succeed. Working families in Pennsylvania are paying their fair share, and wealthy corporations like big oil and gas companies should do the same. Every day Governor Wolf is working to change Harrisburg for all of us -- and he needs our help to keep fighting. "" "" Thanks, Jeff Sheridan Campaign Manager Tom Wolf for Governor Paid for by Tom Wolf for GovernorContributions or gifts to Tom Wolf for Governor are not tax deductible.

name: Tulsi Gabbard, The Sanders Institute
subject: The Gun Control Debate: What Debate?
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE SANDERS INSTITUTE
body:
#
Aloha Friend,
On March 24th, the survivors and families of the Parkland, Florida school shooting will lead supporters in a march on the streets of Washington DC, with others gathering in Hawai'i and communities across the country.
They will call on Congress to make their lives a priority by taking action to pass gun safety legislation that can help prevent mass shootings in America's schools. They want to be heard, and they want action. And we at the Sanders Institute are proud to stand with these courageous young people today and every day.
Enough is enough. The issue of guns in America has turned into a highly partisan one, being used by political parties to stoke divisiveness, rather than actually solve the problem. It's time to put the rhetoric aside and put the safety of our kids, families, and communities first.
[**[READ MY OP-ED]**](https://sandersinstitute-ourrev.bsd.net/page/m/2ba7ac75/bed43eb/5b4fef53/75fe5d4d/289656512/VEsH/ "https://www.sandersinstitute.com/blog/the-gun-control-debate-what-debate")
The vast majority of Americans, those who own guns and those who don't, agree with the actions these students are calling for--banning military-style assault weapons, increasing the minimum age to buy guns, universal background checks, and closing gun show loopholes. These are common sense efforts that can and should be enacted now.
Please join my colleagues and me at the Sanders Institute in doing everything possible to support the March for Our Lives efforts and urging leaders in Congress to take action now to prevent more lives from being lost.
Mahalo,
Rep. Tulsi Gabbard - Congresswoman from Hawai'i
Founding Fellow of The Sanders Institute

disclaimer_text: Paid for by Florida Democratic Party
body:
Team: Tomorrow's March For Our Lives will prove Americans want gun reforms-- but it's also being viciously derided by far-right Republicans here in Florida. [**We 're collecting names to show that Florida Democrats are standing with the Florida students leading this march and all who want to end gun violence. Sign on to show your support.**](http://act.floridadems. "https://act.floridadems.org/page/s/fdp-stand-with-our-student-activists-?firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=34223&city=Englewood&state=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=fldems&utm_content=1+-+Wersquore+collecting+names+to+show+that+&utm_campaign=FDP_EM_PT_180DO_180323_1_2&source=FDP_EM_PT_180DO_180323_1_2&refcode=FDP_EM_PT_180DO_180323_1_2")

_**Begin forwarded message:**_
| **Stand with student activists before the March For Our Lives!**

**[ADD YOUR NAME->](http://act.floridadems. "https://act.floridadems.org/page/s/fdp-stand-with-our-student-activists-?firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=34223&city=Englewood&state=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=fldems&utm_content=2+-+ADD+YOUR+NAME+rarr&utm_campaign=FDP_EM_PT_180DO_180323_1_2&source=FDP_EM_PT_180DO_180323_1_2&refcode=FDP_EM_PT_180DO_180323_1_2")**
---
Friend --

This Saturday, student activists from our own state will be leading the call for real gun reforms with the March For Our Lives.

The NRA and even some elected Florida Republicans have tried to derail this movement, but we know Saturday will prove Americans want an end to the gun epidemic NOW.

[**Parkland student voices are leading the movement for gun reform, and we need to stand by their side and denounce every attempt by far-right extremists to silence them. SIGN YOUR NAME to stand with student activists before the March For Our Lives!**](http://act.floridadems. "https://act.floridadems.org/page/s/fdp-stand-with-our-student-activists-?firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=34223&city=Englewood&state=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=fldems&utm_content=3+-++if+consaddrstateabbr++FL+Parkland+s+els&utm_campaign=FDP_EM_PT_180DO_180323_1_2&source=FDP_EM_PT_180DO_180323_1_2&refcode=FDP_EM_PT_180DO_180323_1_2")

This movement was started by Florida students. They are our future, and they are proving grassroots organizing can promote the change we need!

But the cold and callous remarks against them by those on the extreme-right show that **we have got to vote these Republicans out.**

They're not representing their constituents or America's values. They represent the gun lobby and only the gun lobby.

[**SIGN ON to join us in standing shoulder to shoulder with the students calling for action. Add your name to support the March For Our Lives before Saturday!**](http://act.floridadems. "https://act.floridadems.org/page/s/fdp-stand-with-our-student-activists-?firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=34223&city=Englewood&state=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=fldems&utm_content=4+-+SIGN+ON+to+join+us+in+standing+shoulder+&utm_campaign=FDP_EM_PT_180DO_180323_1_2&source=FDP_EM_PT_180DO_180323_1_2&refcode=FDP_EM_PT_180DO_180323_1_2")

Florida Democrats

name: R. Jai Gillum
subject: They are our future
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Andrew Gillum, Democrat, for Governor
body:
[](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3153/9e00690/455bdcb1/24025bcc/365031255/VEsH/ "https://andrewgillum.com/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=gillum&utm_content=1&utm_campaign=em18_180323_Studnt_engg_gq_act__RJG&source=em18_180323_Studnt_engg_gq_act__RJG")
Friend,

Tomorrow, students, teachers, parents, and millions of other Americans will march in the streets of Washington D.C. and cities all across the country to demand that our elected officials take action to end the epidemic of gun violence.

Turning trauma and an unthinkable tragedy into positive action isn't easy, but the students and teachers of Marjory Stoneman Douglas High School have done exactly that. They have inspired millions of Americans to say #EnoughIsEnough. They've used their platforms to talk about the pervasive gun violence in communities of color, and they've pushed their legislators to change our laws.

Whether you're taking part in a march in your community or standing in solidarity with marchers around the country, you're a part of this movement -- and together we can make sure it doesn't end tomorrow once the marches wrap up.

**[Add your name now to stand in solidarity with everyone participating in the March for Our Lives tomorrow, and then share to spread the word and keep the pressure on.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3153/9e00690/455bdcb1/24025bcf/365031255/VEsE/ "http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/s/add-your-name-support-our-students?utm_campaign=em18_180323_Studnt_engg_gq_act__RJG&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Gillum&source=em18_180323_Studnt_engg_gq_act__RJG&utm_content=2+-+Add+your+name+now+to+stand+in+solidarity")**
[](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3153/9e00690/455bdcb1/24025bce/365031255/VEsF/ "http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/s/add-your-name-support-our-students?utm_campaign=em18_180323_Studnt_engg_gq_act__RJG&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Gillum&source=em18_180323_Studnt_engg_gq_act__RJG&utm_content=3")
I don't want my children to grow up in a world where gun violence is the norm and where active shooter drills are taught alongside math. Our young people are asking us to do something to save their lives -- and it's about time our elected officials listened.

Tomorrow, these young people will be making history and Andrew and I are proud to stand alongside them.

**[Join us in standing in solidarity with the students and marchers tomorrow -- then share and ask your friends and family to do the same. Together, we can make #NeverAgain a reality.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3153/9e00690/455bdcb1/24025bc1/365031255/VEsC/ "http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/s/add-your-name-support-our-students?utm_campaign=em18_180323_Studnt_engg_gq_act__RJG&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Gillum&source=em18_180323_Studnt_engg_gq_act__RJG&utm_content=4+-+Join+us+in+standing+in+solidarity+with+t")**

Thank you,

R. Jai Gillum

Paid for by Andrew Gillum, Democrat, for Governor.

---
Andrew Gillum for Governor
1550 Melvin Street,
Tallahassee, FL 32303

name: Ben Jealous
subject: Young people are pushing back
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF BEN JEALOUS, JASON SMALL, TREASURER
body:
#
Friends,
It's been just over a month since seventeen young people were gunned down inside a Florida high school. And just this week in St. Mary's County, our students faced the terror of yet another school shooting.
But instead of devolving into the same cycle of meaningless debate, we're seeing a new moment of student leadership.
In a time of crushing grief and anger and fear, these students have chosen to rise up and fill the vacuum of leadership that many of our leaders have created. And they've been joined in their activism by their peers all across the country.
**[I know they'll succeed. We have an opportunity this year to elect leaders that won't stand in the way of progress but will usher in the vision these student activists have. If you support these young people's](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a779/7ab0e2c4/7f547f1d/349a7bb5/1114607169/VEsH/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiT0hRMDMyMzhBIn0=/ "http://act.benjealous.com/page/s/march-for-our-lives?source=OHQ03238A")****[ fight to end gun violence, add your name next to mine.](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a779/7ab0e2c4/7f547f1d/349a7bb5/1114607169/VEsE/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiT0hRMDMyMzhBIn0=/ "http://act.benjealous.com/page/s/march-for-our-lives?source=OHQ03238A")**
Our governor has earned an "A-" rating from the NRA but refuses to disclose what he promised them. We can't make real change with leaders lacking the courage it takes to stand up to the NRA and the checks they write.
But these student activists have dedicated themselves to changing the course of history.
The poise, grace, and passion that these students have conducted themselves with have been inspiring. Their movement is a heart-wrenching call to action, a rebirth of student activism which may feel new to us now, but I know the power that student activism can have.
Student activists can be the loudest and most powerful voices. They're not hemmed in by the equivocating and hedging that hampers most political discourse. These students know what will keep them safe and they're not going to stop until they accomplish the change they want to see.
**[The system is broken, but young people are pushing back. Will you add your name in support of their fight?](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a779/7ab0e2c4/7f547f1d/349a7bb5/1114607169/VEsF/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiT0hRMDMyMzhBIn0=/ "http://act.benjealous.com/page/s/march-for-our-lives?source=OHQ03238A")**
I'll be here, doing everything I can to help them.
Onward,
Ben

Friend,

Exciting news.

This morning, Joe was officially endorsed by the United Mine Workers of America.

The mine workers know Joe the way we do -- that he's a champion and a fighter for working people. He never caves to partisan politics.

[](https://secure.joemanchinwv.com/page/m/498198f6/2efbf85c/63c38279/4c92660b/3027668980/VEsE/ "https://www.facebook.com/JoeManchinWV/posts/925222144306030?utm_medium=email&utm_source=manchin&utm_content=2&utm_campaign=JM_EM_EN_180DO_180323&source=JM_EM_EN_180DO_180323&refcode=JM_EM_EN_180DO_180323")

[**_Check out the announcement on Facebook!_**](https://secure.joemanchinwv.com/page/m/498198f6/2efbf85c/63c38279/4c92660a/3027668980/VEsF/ "https://www.facebook.com/JoeManchinWV/posts/925222144306030?utm_medium=email&utm_source=manchin&utm_content=3+-+Check+out+the+announcement+on+Facebook&utm_campaign=JM_EM_EN_180DO_180323&source=JM_EM_EN_180DO_180323&refcode=JM_EM_EN_180DO_180323")
We're proud to have support from coal miners. Joe successfully fought shoulder to shoulder with the UMWA to secure health care benefits for 22,600 coal miners.

Now, Joe is fighting to protect the pensions of West Virginia coal miners. Having the UMWA on our side is huge. It's one of the biggest endorsements in West Virginia.

[**_Share the announcement on Facebook to show Joe you 've got his back!_**](https://secure.joemanchinwv.com/page/m/498198f6/2efbf85c/63c38279/4c926609/3027668980/VEsC/ "https://www.facebook.com/JoeManchinWV/posts/925222144306030?utm_medium=email&utm_source=manchin&utm_content=4+-+Share+the+announcement+on+Facebook+to+sh&utm_campaign=JM_EM_EN_180DO_180323&source=JM_EM_EN_180DO_180323&refcode=JM_EM_EN_180DO_180323")

Joe is proud to join the UMWA in the fight for miner safety, good wages and benefits, and strong representation for working families. We hope that you are too.

Thanks for standing with Joe,

Team Manchin

| **_All 92 Counties_** It's Lincoln Day Dinner season in Indiana! If you've never been to a Lincoln Day event, you're truly missing out.

These great dinners, which are held in each Indiana county (and, in the case of Marion County, each township!), bring together Republicans for great food, great friends, and fascinating speakers. Attend your local Lincoln Day Dinner, and you'll likely hear from speakers ranging from your local elected officials to big-name Republicans like **Governor Eric Holcomb** , **Senator Todd Young** , our members of Congress, and our candidates for U.S. Senate.

Attend some Lincoln Day Dinners, and you might even hear from me. So far this year, I've spoken at Lincoln Day Dinners in Lawrence Township, Newton County, Boone County, Gibson County and (last night) in Adams County. At each event, I'm talking about the big Republican ground game we have in the works, and our plan for defeating Democrat Joe Donnelly in November.

At the core of it all is you -- and the hardworking folks we have on our team in every community of our state. Our statewide success as a party comes from local activity. It comes from you.

Unlike Indiana Democrats, who have a narrow scope of influence in select pockets of our state, your Indiana Republican Party is a true statewide party. Hoosier Republicans like you are hard at work in all of Indiana's 92 counties, where we win elections and deliver on our promises to serve our fellow Hoosiers. Instead of being the party of division and failed policies, we're the Party of Purpose.

[Check here](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=cf3326c7b1&e=1acd9bb3d6) to see when your local event is scheduled. I might even see you there!

-Kyle Hupfer

_Picture: Indiana Republican Party Chairman Kyle Hupfer talks with Adams County Republican Chairman Jerry Walker prior to giving the keynote address at their Lincoln Day Dinner._

| **_Signing Bills, Helping Hoosiers & Building Our Future_**
With the close of the legislative session, **Governor Eric Holcomb** had a busy week, signing important bills that are going to go the distance for Indiana's future!

When it comes to attacking the opioid crisis, yesterday Governor Holcomb signed legislation that will:

**(1)** place maximum felonies on drug dealers when their actions lead to someone's death
**(2)** require that all health care professionals check the state's prescription database before prescribing a potentially-addictive medication
**(3)** place nearly all Hoosiers within an hour's drive of addiction treatment
**(4)** gather better local data on the opioid crisis.

And when it comes to building Indiana's 21st Century workforce, Governor Holcomb also signed two bills that will better align our state's career and technical training programs with the needs of real employers. This new legislation will make sure our workforce programs are accessible for everyone -- including our job creators _and_ our job seekers.

Beyond these two landmark issues, this week Governor Holcomb also signed into law an exemption from state sales tax for software as a service -- which will help spur Indiana's quickly-growing tech industry -- and a new law providing computer training to all Hoosier students.

**NH REPUBLICAN PARTY**
**WEEKLY NEWSLETTER**
**FRIDAY, MARCH 23RD**
|
---
**_A Note From Jeanie_**
**Dear Derek,**

**Whew! What a week!! A visit from President Trump, a visit from Vice President Pence, and a VERY successful fundraiser for Governor Sununu. It's nice to know that New Hampshire matters to our President and Vice President. **

**President Trump visited the Granite State with a message that the opioid crisis is a number one priority and he's a man with a plan to put an end to the scourge that is killing our friends and families. **

[**The plan consists of a three-pronged approach: reduce the demand and over-prescription of opioids, cut off the supply of illegal drugs and boost access to treatment.**](http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=3qP3xl6Iy86fMdiFlFopyOjIesYJFWT3v-2BLoSZZM67AIcexMAnw9MzxNEEiLf3Lm_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30MvV-2BcikI9rmeR5L-2BaByahDN11T5M-2BURL1zTixiZOWXALYwvkTqwVt57P47sStyHKous62WAtLSj5QOHppavAPWUFLI4QpS796Rz-2BCXQyIp2cy-2BqSRdgZcs9rnHHKdGiY6088-2FfYNZXWX-2BP3wS1v-2F7552F8vOnkRQi7EUJzA8zNuTE6-2FvNjuUZTtQc0UzsvBnkeWyQ3gaKFdLihO97-2F4Fxq-2BxXxuHgKY5NLtjaxOd0ATQsJ6xDL56ZbWQSnwhQEXZpoCL15Grrv0wKi5m0bWKGnw-3D-3D)

**The plan aims to reduce the number of opioid prescriptions by one-third over three years. It includes measures that addiction advocates have supported in the past, including increasing access to the gold standard of addiction treatment and incentivizing states to move to a national database monitoring opioid prescriptions to help flag people requesting numerous prescriptions.**

**The plan is also seeking stiffer penalties for high-volume drug traffickers, which includes a mandate to the Department of Justice to seek the death penalty when appropriate under current law. **

**“If we don’t get tough on the drug dealers, we’re wasting our time,” said Trump. I couldn't agree more.**

**Unfortunately, our federal delegation was nowhere in sight --apparently this crisis it not a top priority for them. It's disappointing that Jeanne, Maggie, Carol, and Annie think so little of their constituents; it's shameful that they would rather be partisan instead of partnering with the President to solve this crisis.**

**[Will you let our federal delegation know they've let the people of New Hampshire down with their destructive partisanship?](http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=3qP3xl6Iy86fMdiFlFopyMNJiIkBy3-2FWy62SbsP8USI-3D_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30MvV-2BcikI9rmeR5L-2BaByahDN11T5M-2BURL1zTixiZOWXALYwvkTqwVt57P47sStyHKous62WAtLSj5QOHppavAPWbWWOU0KDGwRmVmBQkvK7KnP5TVkGpjb-2FZwF-2FR3clt9BPLE3gwga1yuWkZfAZO1IFnFu2ry-2FuEGndyrRnaz78uGifpye4QhH8vGWzRIEB0h3pW5P7pGOe3m35ayp4dUgiNC7wtwLK5sHtws3i5PygIzPIByin2DKVDmzRTaKkczqtQUPZXV75FNXXASuqlBivQ-3D-3D)**

**Jeanie**

clean_body: | | nh.gop NH REPUBLICAN PARTY WEEKLY NEWSLETTER FRIDAY, MARCH 23RD | A Note From Jeanie Dear Derek, Whew! What a week!! A visit from President Trump, a visit from Vice President Pence, and a VERY successful fundraiser for Governor Sununu. It's nice to know that New Hampshire matters to our President and Vice President. President Trump visited the Granite State with a message that the opioid crisis is a number one priority and he's a man with a plan to put an end to the scourge that is killing our friends and families. The plan aims to reduce the number of opioid prescriptions by one-third over three years. It includes measures that addiction advocates have supported in the past, including increasing access to the gold standard of addiction treatment and incentivizing states to move to a national database monitoring opioid prescriptions to help flag people requesting numerous prescriptions. The plan is also seeking stiffer penalties for high-volume drug traffickers, which includes a mandate to the Department of Justice to seek the death penalty when appropriate under current law. “If we don’t get tough on the drug dealers, we’re wasting our time,” said Trump. I couldn't agree more. Unfortunately, our federal delegation was nowhere in sight --apparently this crisis it not a top priority for them. It's disappointing that Jeanne, Maggie, Carol, and Annie think so little of their constituents; it's shameful that they would rather be partisan instead of partnering with the President to solve this crisis.
